articles.title: 5 PostgreSQL locking behaviors that trip people up
articles.summary: PostgreSQL's locking system can lead to unexpected delays and outages due to conflicting queries, especially when multiple sessions are operating concurrently. The first example illustrates how an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE request can block other queries in a chain, while the second example shows how foreign key constraints can cause deadlocks by acquiring implicit locks that prevent other transactions from proceeding. These issues can be mitigated by adjusting lock timeouts and monitoring query activity using the pg_stat_activity view.
